name: python-pcapng

metadata.name: python-pcapng
classifiers: License :: OSI Approved :: Apache Software License
metadata.summary: Library to read/write the pcap-ng format used by various packet sniffers.

documentation:
# Python-pcapng

Python library to parse the pcap-ng format used by newer versions
of dumpcap & similar tools (wireshark, winpcap, ...).

## Documentation

If you prefer the RTD theme, or want documentation for any version
other than the latest, head here:

<http://python-pcapng.readthedocs.org/en/latest/>

If you prefer the more comfortable, page-wide, default sphinx theme,
a documentation mirror is hosted on GitHub pages:

<http://rshk.github.io/python-pcapng/>

## CI build status

| Branch  | Status |
| ------- | ------ |
| master  | ```{image} https://travis-ci.org/rshk/python-pcapng.svg?branch=master
:target: https://travis-ci.org/rshk/python-pcapng
```        |
| develop | ```{image} https://travis-ci.org/rshk/python-pcapng.svg?branch=develop
:target: https://travis-ci.org/rshk/python-pcapng
```        |

## Source code

Source, issue tracker etc. on GitHub: <https://github.com/rshk/python-pcapng>

Get the source from git:

```
git clone https://github.com/rshk/python-pcapng
```

Download zip of the latest version:

<https://github.com/rshk/python-pcapng/archive/master.zip>

Install from pypi:

```
pip install python-pcapng
```

## PyPI status

The official page on the Python Package Index is: <https://pypi.python.org/pypi/python-pcapng>

## Why this library?

- I need to decently extract some information from a bunch of pcap-ng
  files, but apparently tcpdump has some problems reading those files,

  I couldn't find other nice tools nor Python bindings to a library
  able to parse this format, so..

- In general, it appears there are (quite a bunch of!) Python modules
  to parse the old (much simpler) format, but nothing for the new one.

- And, they usually completely lack any form of documentation.

## Isn't it slow?

Yes, I guess it would be much slower than something written in C,
but I'm much better at Python than C.

..and I need to get things done, and CPU time is not that expensive :)

(Maybe I'll give a try porting the thing to Cython to speed it up, but
anyways, pure-Python libraries are always useful, eg. for PyPy).

## How do I use it?

Basic usage is as simple as:

```python
from pcapng import FileScanner

with open('/tmp/mycapture.pcap', 'rb') as fp:
    scanner = FileScanner(fp)
    for block in scanner:
        pass  # do something with the block...
```

Have a look at the blocks documentation to see what they do; also, the
`examples` directory contains some example scripts using the library.

## Hacking

Format specification is here:

<https://github.com/pcapng/pcapng/>

Contributions are welcome, please contact me if you're planning to do
some big change, so that we can sort out the best way to integrate it.

Or even better, open an issue so the whole world can participate in
the discussion :)

## Pcap-ng write support

Write support exists as of version 2.0.0. See the file
`examples/generate_pcapng.py` for an example of the minimum code
needed to generate a pcapng file.

In most cases, this library will prevent you from creating broken
data. If you want to create marginal pcapng files, e.g. as test cases
for other software, you can do that by adjusting the "strictness" of
the library, as in:

```python
from pcapng.strictness import Strictness, set_strictness
set_strictness(Strictness.FIX)
```

Recognized values are `Strictness.FORBID` (the default),
`Strictness.FIX` (warn about problems, fix *if possible*),
`Strictness.WARN` (warn only), and `Strictness.NONE` (no warnings).
Circumstances that will result in strictness warnings include:

> - Adding multiples of a non-repeatable option to a block
> - Adding a SPB to a file with more than one interface
> - Writing a PB (PBs are obsolete and not to be used in new files)
> - Writing EPB/SPB/PB/ISB before writing any IDBs

## Creating a release

1. Create a tag for the new version:

   ```
   git tag v2.0.0 -m 'Version 2.0.0'
   ```

2. Install build dependencies in a virtualenv:

   ```
   python -m venv ./.build-venv
   ./.build-venv/bin/python -m pip install build twine
   ```

3. Build source and wheel distributions:

   ```
   rm -rf ./dist *.egg-info
   ./.build-venv/bin/python -m build
   ```

4. Use Twine to upload to pypi:

   ```
   twine upload dist/*
   ```

### Troubleshooting

If you get some crazy version number like
`2.0.1.dev0+g7bd8575.d20220310` instead of what you expect (eg
`2.0.0`), it's because you have uncommitted or untracked files in
your local working copy, or you created more commits after creating
the tag. Such a version number will be refused by pypi (and it's not a
good version number anyways), so make sure you have a clean working
copy before building.

functions.name: pcapng.utils._get_pairs
documentation:
Return data in pairs

This uses a clever hack, based on the fact that zip will consume
items from the same iterator, for each reference found in each
row.

Example:

```
>>> _get_pairs([1, 2, 3, 4])
[(1, 2), (3, 4)]
```

functions.name: pcapng.utils.unpack_timestamp_resolution
documentation:
Unpack a timestamp resolution.

Returns a floating point number representing the timestamp
resolution (multiplier).

functions.name: pcapng.utils.pack_timestamp_resolution
documentation:
Pack a timestamp resolution.

```
param base

2 or 10

param exponent

negative power of the base to be encoded
```

classes.name: pcapng.scanner.FileScanner

methods.name: pcapng.scanner.FileScanner._read_section_header
documentation:
Section information headers are special blocks in that they
modify the state of the FileScanner instance (to change current
section / endianness)

methods.name: pcapng.scanner.FileScanner._read_block
documentation: Read the block payload and pass to the appropriate block constructor

methods.name: pcapng.scanner.FileScanner._read_int
documentation: Read an integer from the stream, using current endianness

pcap-ng file scanner.

This object can be iterated to get blocks out of a pcap-ng
stream (a file or file-like object providing a .read() method).

Example usage:

> ```python
> from pcapng import FileScanner
>
> with open('/tmp/mycapture.pcap', 'rb') as fp:
>     scanner = FileScanner(fp)
>     for block in scanner:
>         pass  # do something with the block...
> ```

```
param stream

a file-like object from which to read the data.
If you need to parse data from some string you have entirely in-memory,
just wrap it in a  object.
```

classes.name: pcapng.exceptions.PcapngException
documentation: Base for all the pcapng exceptions

classes.name: pcapng.exceptions.PcapngWarning
documentation: Base for all the pcapng warnings

classes.name: pcapng.exceptions.PcapngLoadError
documentation: Indicate an error while loading a pcapng file

classes.name: pcapng.exceptions.PcapngDumpError
documentation: Indicate an error while writing a pcapng file

classes.name: pcapng.exceptions.PcapngStrictnessError
documentation: Indicate a condition about poorly formed pcapng files

classes.name: pcapng.exceptions.PcapngStrictnessWarning
documentation: Indicate a condition about poorly formed pcapng files

classes.name: pcapng.exceptions.StreamEmpty
documentation:
Exception indicating that the end of the stream was reached
and exactly zero bytes were read; usually it simply indicates
we reached the end of the stream and no further content is
available for reading.

classes.name: pcapng.exceptions.CorruptedFile
documentation:
Exception used to indicate that something is wrong with the
file structure, possibly due to data corruption.

classes.name: pcapng.exceptions.TruncatedFile
documentation:
Exception used to indicate that not all the required bytes
could be read before stream end, but the read length was
non-zero, indicating a possibly truncated stream.

classes.name: pcapng.exceptions.BadMagic
documentation:
Exception used to indicate a failure due to some bad magic
number encountered (either the file magic or section header
byte order marker).

modules.name: pcapng.blocks
documentation:
Module containing the definition of known / supported "blocks" of the
pcap-ng format.

Each block is a struct-like object with some fields and possibly
a variable amount of "items" (usually options).

They can optionally expose some other properties, used eg. to provide
better access to decoded information, ...

functions.name: pcapng.blocks.register_block
documentation: Handy decorator to register a new known block type

classes.name: pcapng.blocks.Block

methods.name: pcapng.blocks.Block._write
documentation: Writes this block into the given output stream

methods.name: pcapng.blocks.Block._encode
documentation: Encodes the fields of this block into raw data
Base class for blocks

classes.name: pcapng.blocks.SectionMemberBlock
documentation: Block which must be a member of a section

classes.name: pcapng.blocks.SectionHeader

methods.name: pcapng.blocks.SectionHeader.new_member
documentation: Helper method to create a block that's a member of this section

methods.name: pcapng.blocks.SectionHeader.register_interface
documentation: Helper method to register an interface within this section

methods.name: pcapng.blocks.SectionHeader.add_interface_stats
documentation: Helper method to register interface stats within this section

methods.name: pcapng.blocks.SectionHeader.section
documentation: Block.decode() assumes all blocks have sections -- technically true...

"The Section Header Block (SHB) is mandatory. It identifies the beginning
of a section of the capture file. The Section Header Block does not contain
data but it rather identifies a list of blocks (interfaces, packets) that
are logically correlated."
- pcapng spec, section 4.1. Other quoted citations are from this section
unless otherwise noted.

classes.name: pcapng.blocks.InterfaceDescription
documentation:
"An Interface Description Block (IDB) is the container for information
describing an interface on which packet data is captured."
- pcapng spec, section 4.2. Other quoted citations are from this section
unless otherwise noted.

classes.name: pcapng.blocks.BlockWithTimestampMixin
documentation:
Block mixin adding properties to better access timestamps
of blocks that provide one.

classes.name: pcapng.blocks.BlockWithInterfaceMixin
documentation:
Block mixin for blocks that have/require an interface.
This includes all packet blocks as well as InterfaceStatistics.

classes.name: pcapng.blocks.BasePacketBlock

methods.name: pcapng.blocks.BasePacketBlock.packet_len
documentation: Helper function. If the user hasn't explicitly set an original packet
length, use the length of the captured packet data. And if they *have*
set a length but it's smaller than the captured data, make it the same as
the captured data length.

Base class for blocks with packet data.
They must have these fields in their schema:

- `packet_len` is the original amount of data that was "on the wire"
  : (this can differ from `captured_len`)
- `packet_data` is the actual binary packet data (of course)

This class makes the `captured_len` a read-only property returning
the current length of the packet data.

classes.name: pcapng.blocks.EnhancedPacket
documentation:
"An Enhanced Packet Block (EPB) is the standard container for storing the
packets coming from the network."
- pcapng spec, section 4.3. Other quoted citations are from this section
unless otherwise noted.

classes.name: pcapng.blocks.SimplePacket

methods.name: pcapng.blocks.SimplePacket.interface_id
documentation:
"The Simple Packet Block does not contain the Interface ID field.
Therefore, it MUST be assumed that all the Simple Packet Blocks have
been captured on the interface previously specified in the first
Interface Description Block."

methods.name: pcapng.blocks.SimplePacket.captured_len
documentation:
"...the SnapLen value MUST be used to determine the size of the Packet
Data field length."

"The Simple Packet Block (SPB) is a lightweight container for storing the
packets coming from the network."
- pcapng spec, section 4.4. Other quoted citations are from this section
unless otherwise noted.

classes.name: pcapng.blocks.ObsoletePacket

methods.name: pcapng.blocks.ObsoletePacket.enhanced
documentation: Return an EnhancedPacket with this block's attributes.

methods.name: pcapng.blocks.ObsoletePacket._write
documentation: Do this check in _write() instead of _encode() to ensure the block gets written
with the correct magic number.

"The Packet Block is obsolete, and MUST NOT be used in new files. [...] A
Packet Block was a container for storing packets coming from the network."
- pcapng spec, Appendix A. Other quoted citations are from this appendix
unless otherwise noted.

classes.name: pcapng.blocks.NameResolution
documentation:
"The Name Resolution Block (NRB) is used to support the correlation of
numeric addresses (present in the captured packets) and their corresponding
canonical names [...]. Having the literal names saved in the file prevents
the need for performing name resolution at a later time, when the
association between names and addresses may be different from the one in
use at capture time."
- pcapng spec, section 4.5. Other quoted citations are from this section
unless otherwise noted.

classes.name: pcapng.blocks.InterfaceStatistics
documentation:
"The Interface Statistics Block (ISB) contains the capture statistics for a
given interface [...]. The statistics are referred to the interface defined
in the current Section identified by the Interface ID field."
- pcapng spec, section 4.6. Other quoted citations are from this section
unless otherwise noted.

classes.name: pcapng.blocks.UnknownBlock
documentation:
Class used to represent an unknown block.

Its block type and raw data will be stored directly with no further
processing.

modules.name: pcapng
documentation: Library to parse pcap-ng file format
See: http://www.winpcap.org/ntar/draft/PCAP-DumpFileFormat.html

modules.name: pcapng.strictness
documentation:
Module for alerting the user when attempting to do things with pcapng that
aren't strictly valid.

class_variables.name: pcapng.strictness.Strictness.NONE
documentation: No warnings, do what you want

class_variables.name: pcapng.strictness.Strictness.WARN
documentation: Do what you want, but warn of potential issues

class_variables.name: pcapng.strictness.Strictness.FIX
documentation: Warn of potential issues, fix *if possible*

class_variables.name: pcapng.strictness.Strictness.FORBID
documentation: raise exception on potential issues

functions.name: pcapng._compat.namedtuple
documentation: version-portable namedtuple with defaults

modules.name: pcapng.constants.link_types
documentation: Canonical list maintained at https://www.tcpdump.org/linktypes.html
Some of the reserved values integrated with
http://www.winpcap.org/ntar/draft/PCAP-DumpFileFormat.html#appendixLinkTypes
No link layer information. A packet saved with this link layer
contains a raw L3 packet preceded by a 32-bit host-byte-order AF_
value indicating the specific L3 type.

documentation: Generic contsnts

modules.name: pcapng.flags
documentation: Module to wrap an integer in bitwise flag/field accessors.

classes.name: pcapng.flags.FlagBase
documentation:
Base class for flag types to be used in a Flags object.
Handles the bitwise math so subclasses don't have to worry about it.

classes.name: pcapng.flags.FlagBool
documentation: Object representing a single boolean flag

classes.name: pcapng.flags.FlagUInt
documentation:
Object representing an unsigned integer of the given size stored in
a larger bitfield

classes.name: pcapng.flags.FlagEnum
documentation:
Object representing a range of values stored in part of a larger
bitfield

classes.name: pcapng.flags.FlagWord

methods.name: pcapng.flags.FlagWord.__init__
documentation:
---
param initial: The initial integer value of the flags field
param nbits: An integer representing the total number of bits used for flags
param schema: |-
  A list of FlagField objects representing the values to be packed
  into this object, in order from LSB to MSB of the underlying int
---
Class to wrap an integer in bitwise flag/field accessors.

classes.name: pcapng.writer.FileWriter

methods.name: pcapng.writer.FileWriter.__init__
documentation:
Start writing a new pcap-ng section to the given stream. Writes the
{py:class}`SectionHeader` immediately. Also writes any
{py:class}`InterfaceDescription` blocks that have been created for
the section.

```
param stream

a file-like object to which to write the data.

param shb

a  to start the section.
```

methods.name: pcapng.writer.FileWriter.write_block
documentation:
Write the given block to this stream.

If the block is a {py:class}`pcapng.blocks.SectionHeader`, then a new
section will be started in the same output stream, along with any
{py:class}`InterfaceDescription` blocks that have been created for the section.

```
param blk

a  to write.
```
pcap-ng file writer.

modules.name: pcapng.structs
documentation: Module providing facilities for handling struct-like data.

functions.name: pcapng.structs.read_int
documentation:
Read (and decode) an integer number from a binary stream.

```
param stream

an object providing a read() method

param size

the size, in bits, of the number to be read.
Supported sizes are: 8, 16, 32 and 64 bits.

param signed

Whether a signed or unsigned number is required.
Defaults to False (unsigned int).

param endianness

specify the endianness to use to decode the number,
in the same format used by Python  module.
Defaults to '=' (native endianness). '!' means "network" endianness
(big endian), '<' little endian, '>' big endian.

return

the read integer number
```

functions.name: pcapng.structs.write_int
documentation:
Write (and encode) an integer number to a binary stream.

```
param number

the integer number to write

param stream

an object providing a write() method

param size

the size, in bits, of the number to be written.
Supported sizes are: 8, 16, 32 and 64 bits.

param signed

Whether a signed or unsigned number is required.
Defaults to False (unsigned int).

param endianness

specify the endianness to use to encode the number,
in the same format used by Python  module.
Defaults to '=' (native endianness). '!' means "network" endianness
(big endian), '<' little endian, '>' big endian.
```

functions.name: pcapng.structs.read_section_header
documentation:
Read a section header block from a stream.

:::{note}
The byte order magic will be removed from the returned data
This is ok as we don't need it anymore once we determined
the correct endianness of the section.
:::

```
returns

a dict containing the 'endianness' and 'data' keys
that will be used to construct a 
instance.
```

functions.name: pcapng.structs.read_block_data
documentation:
Read block data from a stream.

Each "block" is in the form:

- 32bit integer indicating the size (including header and size)
- block payload (the above-specified number of bytes minus 12)
- 32bit integer indicating the size (again)

```
param stream

the stream from which to read data

param endianness

Endianness marker, one of '<', '>', '!', '='.
```

functions.name: pcapng.structs.read_bytes
documentation:
Read the given amount of raw bytes from a stream.

```
param stream

the stream from which to read data

param size

the size to read, in bytes

returns

the read data

raises

 if zero bytes were read

raises

 if 0 < bytes < size
were read
```

functions.name: pcapng.structs.write_bytes
documentation:
Write the given amount of raw bytes to a stream.

```
param stream

the stream into which to write data

param data

the data to write
```

functions.name: pcapng.structs.read_bytes_padded
documentation:
Read the given amount of bytes from a stream, plus read and discard
any necessary extra byte to align up to the pad_block_size-sized
next block.

```
param stream

the stream from which to read data

param size

the size to read, in bytes

returns

the read data

raises

 if zero bytes were read

raises

 if 0 < bytes < size
were read
```

functions.name: pcapng.structs.write_bytes_padded
documentation:
Read the given amount of bytes from a stream, plus read and discard
any necessary extra byte to align up to the pad_block_size-sized
next block.

```
param stream

the stream from which to read data

param size

the size to read, in bytes

returns

the read data

raises

 if zero bytes were read

raises

 if 0 < bytes < size
were read
```

functions.name: pcapng.structs.read_options
documentation:
Read "options" from an "options block" in a stream, until a
`StreamEmpty` exception is caught, or an end marker is reached.

Each option is composed by:

- option_code (uint16)
- value_length (uint16)
- value (value_length-sized binary data)

The end marker is simply an option with code `0x0000` and no payload

functions.name: pcapng.structs.write_options
documentation:
Each option is composed by:

- option_code (uint16)
- value_length (uint16)
- value (value_length-sized binary data)

The end marker is simply an option with code `0x0000`, length 0,
and no payload

functions.name: pcapng.structs.struct_decode
documentation:
Decode structured data from a stream, following a schema.

```
param schema

a list of three tuples: (name, field, default), where name is a
string representing the attribute name, and field is an instance of
a  sub-class, providing a .load() method to
be called on the stream to get the field value. default is used
when manually instantiating a block, but is ignored here.

param stream

a file-like object, providing a .read() method, from which data
will be read.

param endianness

endianness specifier, as accepted by Python struct module
(one of <>!=, defaults to =).

return

a dictionary mapping the field names to decoded data
```

functions.name: pcapng.structs.struct_encode
documentation: Encode structured data to a stream.

variables.name: pcapng.structs.TYPE_U8
documentation: Unsigned integer, 8 bits

variables.name: pcapng.structs.TYPE_I8
documentation: Signed integer, 8 bits

classes.name: pcapng.structs.StructField
documentation: Abstract base class for struct fields

classes.name: pcapng.structs.RawBytes
documentation:
Field containing a fixed-width amount of raw bytes

```
param size

field size, in bytes
```

classes.name: pcapng.structs.IntField
documentation:
Field containing an integer number.

```
param size

number size, in bits. Currently supported
are 8, 16, 32 and 64-bit integers

param signed

whether the number is a signed or unsigned
integer. Defaults to False (unsigned)
```

classes.name: pcapng.structs.OptionsField
documentation:
Field containing some options.

```
param options_schema

Same as the schema parameter to  class
constructor.
```

classes.name: pcapng.structs.PacketBytes
documentation:
Field containing some "packet data", used in the Packet
and EnhancedPacket blocks.

The packet data is composed of three fields (returned in a tuple):

- captured len (uint32)
- packet len (uint32)
- packet data (captured_len-sized binary data)

classes.name: pcapng.structs.ListField
documentation:
A list field is a variable amount of fields of some other type.
Used for packets containing multiple "items", such as
{py:class}`~pcapng.blocks.NameResolution`.

It will keep loading data using a subfield until a
{py:exc}`~pcapng.exceptions.StreamEmpty` excaption is raised, indicating
we reached the end of data (note that a sub-field might even "simulate"
a stream end once it reaches some end marker in the file).

Values are returned in a list.

```
param subfield

a  sub-class instance to be
used to read values from the stream.
```

classes.name: pcapng.structs.NameResolutionRecordField
documentation:
A name resolution record field contains an item of data used in
the {py:class}`~pcapng.blocks.NameResolution` block.

it is composed of three fields:

- record type (uint16)
- record length (uint16)
- payload

Accepted types are:

- `0x00` - end marker
- `0x01` - ipv4 address resolution
- `0x02` - ipv6 address resolution

In both cases, the payload is composed of a valid address in the
selected IP version, followed by null-separated/terminated domain names.

classes.name: pcapng.structs.EPBFlags
documentation: Class representing the epb_flags option on an EPB

classes.name: pcapng.structs.Options

methods.name: pcapng.structs.Options.get_all
documentation: Get all values for the given option

methods.name: pcapng.structs.Options.get_raw
documentation: Get raw value for the given option

methods.name: pcapng.structs.Options.get_all_raw
documentation: Get all raw values for the given option

methods.name: pcapng.structs.Options.iter_all_items
documentation:
Similar to {py:meth}`iteritems` but will yield a list of values
as the second tuple field.

methods.name: pcapng.structs.Options.add
documentation: Add a value to the given-named option

methods.name: pcapng.structs.Options._check_multiples
documentation: Check if a non-repeatable option is repeated

Wrapper object used to easily access the contents of an "options"
field.

Fields can be accessed either by numerical id or by name (if one was
specified in the schema).

:::{note}
When iterating the object (or calling {py:meth}`keys` /
{py:meth}`iterkeys` / {py:meth}`viewkeys`) string keys will be
returned if possible in place of numeric keys. (The purpose of this
is to achieve better readability, for example, when converting
to a dictionary).
:::

```
param schema

Definition of the known options: a list of Option objects.

The following value types are currently supported:

string: convert value to a unicode string, using utf-8 encoding

{u,i}{8,16,32,64}: (un)signed integer of the specified length

ipv4: a single ipv4 address [4 bytes]

ipv4+mask: an ipv4 address followed by a netmask [8 bytes]

ipv6: a single ipv6 address [16 bytes]

ipv6+prefix: an ipv6 address followed by prefix length [17 bytes]

macaddr: a mac address [6 bytes]

euiaddr: a eui address [8 bytes]

epb_flags: 32-bit bitmask as per pcapng spec section 4.3.1

type+bytes: field where the first byte is a type, and
the remainder is bytes

opt_custom_str and opt_custom_bytes: 4 bytes of Private
Enterprise Number, followed by str or bytes (see pcapng spec,
section 3.5.1)

param data

Initial data for the options. A dict of code: value items.
Items with the same code may be repeated; only the first one will be
accessible using subscript options[code]; the others can be
accessed using  and related methods

param endianness

The current endianness of the section these options came from.
Required in order to load numeric fields.
```
